reviews.title: Look for another place to stay

The room was dirty, and I found a used toothpick and floss on the floor. The internet or Wi-Fi connection was poor.  I would not recommend this hotel. Go elsewhere or find near Alona or Libaong beach. Tons of other places to stay.

reviews.title: Avoid Conrada's Dirty Old Hotel
reviews.text:
Completely overpriced. 

Despite having a wall and a gate on the front of the property the facility is not at all secure.  There is not even a fence on the back of the property. Anybody can walk up and enter any of the rooms. 

The rooms have cheap key lock knobs with no deadbolt. The windows are easy to bypass, jealousy windows. 

We requested a King size bed but instead we got a queen size bed and a mattress on the floor.

Very slow WIFI. The hotel is in a dead zone for our cell phones. 

The staff barely spoke to us even at check in & out. 

The towels are thin and cheap. The material for the towel came off on my body when I dried off. This required me to wash off again. 

The location is isolated & hard to find. There is a sign indicating the location But the sign is so old and faded it cannot be read. 

Agoda said this room was 1,800 pesos then with fees the final price was 3,500. This room is not worth that price.

reviews.title: Nor worth it
reviews.text:
1. Location is very far from the main gig.  You're gonna have to pay 150 tuktuk to get to the beach everytime
2. Night time was not safe for families. There's only one staff available during night time and was not able to assist on your needs
3. Too much chemical on the pool that leads to immediate wrinkle on the skin of the kida. The staff admitted that there's too much chemical being applied there. There was also a smell and taste of iron in their pool. The rusty one.
4. The rooms lighting are always dimmed  until i told the owner about the issue, only did they replaces the bulbs.
5. The owner kept on saying the place was safe but i guarantee it was not. It was an urban over an urban over an urban with growling wolf flavors i  the night although i know those are just dogs
6. Thw breakfast served was something you can buy for 20-30 pesos outside (less than a dollar worth)
7. The room wasnt properly cleaned up from the previous guest. We even saw ashtrays filled with cigarette butts
8. You'll have a lamp shade on your bed side table but surprisingly, there's no plug in there so it doesnt work
9.the owner talks like its more of the profit that she was after rather than the service.
10. The staffs even agreed with my observations

reviews.title: Terrible Experience, Not Safe for Kids
reviews.text:
My kid got into an accident in the step in the pool due to slippery/dirty tiles. I don't know if they are cleaning the pool. He had 5 stitches, which I had to shoulder by myself. The management of the pool didn't even provide transportation. It took as an hour before we managed to bring my kid to the clinic.

The location of the resort is very scary. It's very far from the center and you had to take a bumpy muddy road. It's actually the end of the road and very scary at night. There are not much lights in the resort.  You cannot walk the road because of too many dogs that are very aggressive.

I definitely don't suggest bringing your family here if you don't want to experience the same nightmare that we experienced.

reviews.title: Only if you need a family room!
reviews.text: This hotel is far from the road down a muddy path big enough for 1 vehicle.  Walking through mud is not an adventure. 20 minute walk to the beach and 15 minutes to the main area of Panglao for meals. There is nothing at the hotel or near to eat. Breakfast 1 day was 1 egg, rice, slice of watermelon. Day 2 was slice of ham, 1 egg, toast a slice of pineapple. No juice or brewed coffee  with fresh milk at all. The rooms were 4 walls and 2 beds. No bedside lamps, 1 outlet to plug a cell phone and another for a ref. The TV was useless since there is no cable but the lady said you don't come here to watch tv. So it's perfect if you sleep at 9pm. The bathroom has no toiletries so bring your own. The towels we had to ask for and it's polyester so don't expect to dry yourself. We had to ask for tp also. The bed was relatively comfortable but the sheets were stained and had holes in them. The room had an unpleasant odor like it was close to a septic tank. The pool looked clean but knowing how cheap it is there I didn't see the filter/pump working. I could go on but I would no stay here again. The only reason we stayed here was the family size room with 2 beds, which seems hard to find. Read other reviews and everything negative is true. Most of the positive reviews don't seem to have comments so I don't understand what the positive might be. You can trust me or live it for yourself.

reviews.title: Not what you expect
reviews.text:
Staff are nice only if you find one. 
Lobby or receptionist closed at night so there is no one to help you if need you need anything such as toiletries. They said we can ask the guards but they didn't know either. 
The resort itself is very old, bathroom are dirty, water runs really low. Cabinets are broken. Tv hardly works. Refrigerator hardly works. 
They only start cleaning the pool on our 3rd day. 
Breakfast are free but not something you'll look forward. They were bread and hotdog almost everyday. 
And when we asked to change room we had to pay extra for upgrade. 
You won't be able to sleep in their annex area, the dogs are howling all night long, yes dogs!  A lot of them. Never ever booking this place again. Review were not honest. They probably put a good review themselves.

reviews.title: Disappointing Stay with Poor Management and Hygiene Issues
reviews.text:
The hotel has great potential, but it is evident that better management is necessary to improve the guest experience. Unfortunately, our stay was far from satisfactory.  

Our room was in a disappointing state upon arrival. It was dirty and damp, as if it had been cleaned hastily. The bathroom was particularly unpleasant—the floor was dirty, the toilet seat had mold stains all over it, and the toilet flush was not working well. The shower curtain was dirty and damp, and there was hair on the floor from a previous guest. The kitchenette sink had a small piece of tissue paper stuck in the drainage, making it unhygienic and unsightly. The stains throughout the room were off-putting, making the overall experience uncomfortable and unwelcoming.  

Adding to the issues, the water supply was unreliable during our stay. On one occasion, we were left without water overnight and had to wait until after breakfast for it to be restored, which was extremely inconvenient.  

The breakfast area was another disappointment. It was disorganized, and the staff showed little interest in accommodating guests' preferences. When we requested plain scrambled eggs, we were told it couldn’t be done and were served runny eggs instead, which not everyone enjoys.  

Overall, this was the worst hotel experience we’ve had in all our travels, including visits to various regions of the Philippines. With proper attention to cleanliness, organization, and customer service, this hotel could be much better. However, as it stands, it falls far short of acceptable standards.

reviews.text:

Walang makakainan na malapit sa area. Makitid ang daan papasok sa hotel tapos d pa maliwag,dapat naka
Lagay talaga kong ilang minutes lalakarin from
Hotel to beach. Hindi advisable lalo na pag my
Kasama kayong senior 🥲
Pag nasa hotel ka tatagain ka nga mga charges. 
Wag kayo mag request ng sasakyan pa airport sobrang mahal nila mag paton ng charge. Kong sa labas pweding 100 pero sa kanila 500 to 700 per tricycle only.

reviews.title: A nightmare to get used to
reviews.text: The owners are looking to sell but the whole place needs alot of maintenance. The door to our room was more of an obstacle to us than anyone trying to break in as it took 5 minutes to unlock the door where it would of been easier to just kick it open. The number of ants in the room was crazy and the aircon needed maintenance or replacement.

reviews.title: Inconsistent Policy
reviews.text:
The property is located in an inner alley, the road leading to the hotel is without street light and not cemented. Nightmare if you dont have your own personal car, the road would be muddy. The hotel policy seems inconsistent with what is published in the booking site! 
The owner charged us extra,  and even threatened to call the police! i just paid extra and just  kept quiet since I was that very tired after a long flight! 
The cost is fair, but not worth the hazzle.

reviews.title: Surprise:(
reviews.text: Really 180 degrees fromwhat I see in the website versus the actual place. Need a well thought out plan to clean the place. Cigarette butts full in a pot! Smelly and the shower curtain is moldy and smells! Wifi not so great too! Really disappointed that it does not reflect whats in the website!

reviews.title: Hair and fingernails in bedding
reviews.text: This place would not be so bad if they just cleaned it properly.  I know it’s the Philippines and you can’t expect luxery for the price but when your going to bed and there is other people’s hair everywhere in the sheets  it kind of makes it hard to relax.  The pictures make it look so nice but it definitely isn’t a pool that you want to hang out around.  Very run down and dirty. Friendly staff and comfortable bed but the dirty sheets was a big no for me.

reviews.title: Really disappointed
reviews.text: On arrival there was hair in the sink [lots of hairs] in the bathroom sink. There was food scraps in the kitchen sink from previous tenants. There were no towels so we requested and received old towels. We decided to use the pool, there was tissue and other items floating in the mirky water I wouldn't put my head underwater but the man clean it this morning. The door is quite not properly working' tv  not working. Only one chair in the balcony. Should i go on.

reviews.title: Misleading
reviews.text: It says it’s near the beach but there’s no possible way to get to the beach besides going all the way around which goes down a sketchy long (dark at night)road . Not really near any tourist spots… you will do a lot of walking and needing to rent a tricycle if you stay here … Restroom had a really bad smell. Our room was not where we expected it to be like as seen on the post, we were put in a whole different building/ area …

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: It is not worth it.

reviews.title: Local quaint hotel.
reviews.text: Pictures deceiving. Much smaller place then picture reveals. Quaint but out of the way. You want seclusion then this is the place for you. Long walk to beach. Great staff and owner but was not really our cup of tea!

reviews.title: Poor service and facilities
reviews.text: They are severely understaffed, the owner was hostile, and the facilities aren’t that clean. The free breakfast was just okay. It was not a good value for the money.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Standard room does not have hot shower (, i think the water heater is defective) , only 1 tv channel in our first night, gate doesn't have lock  The location is far from alona beach,  approximately 15 minutes walk. I personally dont recommend.

reviews.title: Not recommendable
reviews.text: We spent one night with a broken door lock. I was asking for a room change but was denied due to unavailability; even though I can tell that the  hotel is only 10-20% occupied at that time. I had to be creative to make sure that the door wouldn't be easy to open from the outside. The location is far from the beach. The room had a certain smell that is not pleasant. The power outlets comes in pair: one with adapter (Pinoy friendly) and the other is not (Europlug - type C or F???) On the other hand, shout out to Mr. Julius for  being accommodating when serving breakfast. The breakfast is good. Props to Manong Guard for allowing us to leave our things in the veranda (after checkout) while we were roaming around while we were waiting for our flight back. Overall, I would not return to this place and would never recommend to family and friends.

reviews.title: Running water tasted salty.
reviews.text: Running water tasted salty. Why is that? You dont feel clean and fresh after taking a bath and after brushing your teeth. AC cant overpower the low temp inside the room.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: add on person is too pricey,  and add on time also

reviews.title: Garbage hotel
reviews.text: NEVER, NEVER, NO, NO NO DO NOT BOOK THIS HOTEL, MOLD, DIRTY, nasty, moldy, nothing works, request a refund.  if your from USA you will hate this hotel WARNING!!!!!

reviews.title: too far and old
reviews.text:
This Swedish-owned hotel seems to have had its best days 20 years ago. The brochure images are misleading and don’t reflect the current state. The room was large, which is a plus, but that’s about it. Issues included a leaking fridge, very low water pressure in the shower, a super noisy aircon, and bugs in the room. To make it worse, there was no cleaning on the second day.

The location is near the beach, but there’s no direct access—you’ll need a moto or taxi. The road is narrow, dark, and floods easily after rain. Sadly, the overall experience was very disappointing. Even when leaving, the owner called a tuk-tuk for me, but took a little extra tip for herself in the process. Avoid this place.

reviews.title: Very bad wifi
reviews.text: Shower did not work. Wi-Fi was so bad. AC is very noisy

reviews.title: Superior room?
reviews.text: This place needs refurbishing, the rooms were dirty, furniture broken, we booked superior rooms expecting something special but was very disappointed, tv didn’t work, no choice at breakfast, quite a long walk to the beach, we were only booked for 2 nights fortunately!

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: The restroom outside is VERYYYY DIRTY. IT’S VERY WEIRD THAT THERE ARE POOP ALL OVER THE FLOOR. IT EVEN HAS WORM WHICH IS VERY GROSS. THE POOL IS VERY DIRTY AS WELL. THE WATER IS NOT EVEN CLEAR. I DONT RECOMMEND THIS HOTEL

reviews.title: Almost nice
reviews.text: The owner and staff were nice.  And the location was ok.  I feel that the photos I saw on the website were heavily photoshopped.  The room was not like the photos.  It was dirty.  The bed was comfortable.  There was a little rug floating around that maybe was a bath mat.  But seemed dirty and gross.  There were no towels in the room twice during our stay.  And they were very old.  I had lint all over my body using them.  There was breakfast but No choice of items.  And no restaurant.  If they spent 10% more on expenses it would have been a much better value.  I would not stay there again.

reviews.title: Too far from front beach
reviews.text: Need to ride a tricycle every time you need to go to Alona Beach. Too dark if will walk to and from the beach since no street lights during the night. No available inhouse restaurant too for lunch or dinner.

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.text: There are much better places for that budget

reviews.title: Bed Bugs
reviews.text: There are bed bugs. We got biten and it is worst. The hotel is cheap and looks clean but still i don’t recommend it.

reviews.title: Good & Bad (Mostly bad)
reviews.text:
The hotel is quite far from Alona beach (15min walk at least). It's also located down a long, dimly lit alley so could be quite dangerous if you are alone at night.

Let's start with the good:
Nice swimming pool
Friendly staff 
Motorbike hire

Negatives:

Dirty room - fridge was extremely dirty.
Room - Taps hanging off. No mugs for coffee.
Very overpriced. also their late check-out fee is 500p per hour!
Free breakfast until 10am, however I went there at 9:25 and the staff said they stopped serving. 
Hotel advertises pickup from airport - I sent several emails to arrange this but they never replied.

reviews.title: Door Locks Broken, Only 1 Electricity Adaptor
reviews.text: The room is spacious but dim and feels EMPTY, only very basic things were provided, door locks is broken , good thing we left our laptops at home, and ONLY 1 ADAPTOR for electricity. So we have to choose ONLY ONE what to plug, a TV, a water heater, a fan, or a cellphone.

reviews.title: Staff are not well accomodating
reviews.text:
The place are a bit far from alona beach. It will take approximately 20-25 minutes of walking to reach there. There’s an option for you to take tricycle but they will ask for a big amount of fare. The staff there are not friendly enough except for the kitchen crew. Breakfast is nice which is very common (tocino, longganisa, egg, hotdog etc..) Just be mindful about their tour package. Based on our experience, you will find it cheaper if you will ask the people outside especially near the beach because you negotiate for a much lower price than them and it’s a big difference. Lastly, the security guard gives me a bad impression because he used to smoke in his station wherein there is designated smoking area yet he is not wearing his upper uniform properly and he also giving favor to other foreigner to smoke as well to an area where he wanted to. 
Overall, one time stay is enough. We will not come back again.
reviews.response: Hi, Marick! Sorry for the trouble faced. We have duly noted your feedback and will ensure better services in the future. Have a nice day ahead :) Regards, Kevin

reviews.title: would not come again
reviews.text: in general the place does not look bad at first glance. however, that changes right after you enter the premises. the deluxe room has nothing to o with deluxe. the only difference from standard room is the small fridge available. thats it! moreover, they have 2 different buildings so if you are in the one out of the main buidling you have to go to the reception/restaurant/coffee/pool everytime. no telephone or communication channel available. honestly, right opposite this place is two new places with new, fresh, nice houses/apartments/rooms and very nice staff. moreover one of them is promoting the night for 2000php as starting price, because they are newly opened and obviously need customers. that is how business is made! conradas place should do some serious research in their neighborhood, otherwise they will just simply crash in few years. never again. worst stay in panglao so far for me and my family. and that was 3rd time in a year. sorry not sorry

reviews.title: Security and staff are peeping tom's
reviews.text: We had 2 rooms for 2 couples, my mates room had a small window with no curtain that was in a closed off maintenance area.  The third night my friends wife came out of the shower and there were 3 Filipino men looking thru this window watching her!  Of course they ran away when my mate went to investigate.  We confronted management about this and their response was they had a report that a woman was in distress in the room and the Security Guard was only checking on her welfare!  Why did it take three men to check?  And haven't they heard of knocking on the door!

reviews.title: disappointing but okay
reviews.text: great for a budget stay but wifi is so slow, there’s no staff at night, hotel isn’t responsive - also it’s a long walk from alona beach!

reviews.title: 3D2N STAY
reviews.text:
Me and my friend stayed in Conrada's for 3 days and 2 nights. 

Room: Good for Family stay since the room was spacious and there was sink, mini ref, and hot shower. However, the light was dim and the ambiance was just so-so.

Staff: We felt like the staff were very welcoming to foreigners but not very friendly to us, normal Filipino citizens. We made a request and we heard other staff declined.

Food: Breakfast food was delicious. We hope that they have more coffee choices rather than black coffee.

Distance: Not so near the center and the tricab drivers were offering overpriced transpo fee. 

Overall, our stay in the hotel was just average. We spent a lot of time outside coz we didn't feel like staying in the place for a long time. The wifi was very slow also.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: Not what I expected. Disappointed. We left after 1 night stay and our accommodations was for 2nights stay. TV not working no remote, rusted towel bar, door hard to open, rusty water and breakfast was mediocre.  Luckily we found another place that was available.

reviews.title: August Visit
reviews.text: While this resort is relatively cheap compared to others within/near the seaside, the rooms are not well-cleaned when we checked-in - there's an empty Heineken in one of the rooms. During our 3D2N stay, the water pressure from the faucet and shower is quite low or there's no water at all --maybe since we're on the second floor. Also, the room is not cold enough since there's only two small aircon unit --- one in the main bedroom (new) and one in main hall (old), but there's no aircon just electric and exhaust fans in the third room. Nonetheless, it was a great accom if you'll spend most of your time outside.

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text:
What a let down for me and my family and waste of money. I booked this place 7 bedrooms. The facility has two locations; they are just walking distance from the other. Each location has its own swimming pool. We were in the location where the pool is just like a slice of pizza, too little. The location is too far away from the beach.  If free breakfast is a plus for you, I don’t call it breakfast myself.  Most of the staff are nice, but the guard or caretaker was upset when we have to wake him up to open the locked gate when we got back from the beach at around 1 am. 
When I go back on vacation will definitely not coming back in this place.

reviews.title: Honest review
reviews.text:
1. Not responsive to questions thru FB page. You have to call the owner directly thru her mobile. 
2. We arrived early (8am) they don’t allow us to check in early, but they allow us to leave our luggages at the reception area. Then we went to the beach and wait for a couple of hours there for us the check in and rest. They are strict in Check in / check out time. 
3. Beach is far, so if you’re in your 50’s, this hotel is not recommended because you will have to walk more or less 1.5 kilometers just to reach the Alona Beach. TriCab is only available at the hi-way. You can ride the TriCab going back to the hotel. 
4. Free breakfast: We stayed for almost a week, and everyday they only served one set plated meal for breakfast,  1 hotdog, 1 egg, sliced of watermelon, bread and coffee. (We ate the same set of breakfast for 5 days) medyo naumay kami😥 
5. Pool: upon arrival we were a bit disappointed with the cleanliness, especially the pool that colored green, at first, I thought it was a fish pond. I didn’t swim there during our stay. 
6. The Staff: they were all kind and approachable. They attended to our request to change beddings and towels. 
7. Room: we availed the de luxe room, it was spacious with a mini ref, water dispenser and electric kettle. Our Comfort room has broken door lock. It’s not an issue to us since we’re couple.
8.  Food: They don’t serve food except breakfast. So you have to go outside the hotel, walk to the hi-way where there’s a lot of restaurants to buy food or eat there.  Food delivery is not always available. By the way, there also nearby hotels that serve food, but a bit pricey. 
9. Peaceful. Since this hotel is far from Alona Beach, the surroundings are quiet during night time. You can sleep without the disturbing sounds of the pub. 
10.  Overall : This is just an average hotel.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: The accommodation was not worth the money paid. The biggest disadvantage is that this hotel is very far from the beach (more than 1 kilometer), it is definitely not 440 meters as promissed. Hotel is in kind of dark area very far from everything. Locker to the balcony was not working, it was impossible to lock the door. Wi-fi had big problems, it was not working very offen. For 3 nighs (couple) we got only 3 shampoos (so only for 1 person). Breakfast was very small and pretty the same every day.

reviews.title: The photos don’t capture the bugs crawling around
reviews.text:
-there are little bugs all over the place 
-the air conditioner is really loud, but protects you from mosquitos
-free breakfast is repulsive
-one towel / cheap soap

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: It's a little bit far going to the beach and other stablishments

reviews.title: Calm
reviews.text:
We stayed in a standard room for one night. Room is big and it’s calm. Building is nice. Not far from Alona beach.

However my son doesn’t enjoyed the pool because it’s dirty . There’s a lot of mosquitoes outside during the night .Not enough lights in the room.Wifi is weak.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Staff were all  very nice.  Price is affordable.  Poor air conditioning but they provided fan. Door need fixing, didn’t feel safe with the doors.  I didn’t like the location,  far from the beach.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Super layo sa kabihasnan and ang patay ng itsura ng hotel but pwede na sa mag stay lang naman saglit sa hotel. But not recommended

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: AC just good enough. Sometimes hot sometimes cool. I had two reservations 1 for a deluxe room and one for a standard room The standard room reservation disappeared but they had another deluxe room for extra.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Wifi was terrible and did not even work well. The room i was in had bed bugs. I got bit many times on my legs and thighs. The food for breakfast was good and the staff were friendly Nice, and accommodating. Unfortunately Because of the terrible internet and bed bugs i cannot recommend that anyone else get a room here as the bed bugs are un acceptable. The room needs to have some serious pest control services. I had to remove all my clothes and keep them in plastic bags so that the bed bugs don't spread to my home. I will have to place them in a hot water and then put them in a hot dryer so that the bed bugs will die.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Ok nman ang place. Pwede na for the price. Di lang namin inexpect malayo sya sa beach. Mahirap pa sumakay. Sira2 ung pinto ng CR. Mahina ung water. Pool is good. Bfrast pwede na.

reviews.title: Not recommended
reviews.text: It’s not in a good location for us , doesn’t look like pics either . Our room only had 2 outlets, none by the sink or rest room . Only one plug by the tv that worked so you couldn’t hv the tv and fridge working on that plug  uggg. Then only one by the bed so it wasn’t easy to use them . No plug for the hot water for coffee on the cabinet by the sink . Had to move it to the refrigerator outlet and unplug it to use . We won’t stay again .

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Good place but not so convenient for its distance from the beach...

reviews.title: Too expensive for what you get
reviews.text: Clean sheets and toilet, but the rest dirty and dusty. Filipino breakfast only - cold rice, cold egg and sausage. Cold shower. Pool not cleaned. There was a very nice member of staff who served breakfast who tried to accomodate us europeans as much as possible.

reviews.title: Disappointing!!!
reviews.text:
Avoid this hotel. While the rate is very attractive, the rooms very spacious and its staff very friendly and attentive, it is extremely inconvenient. Its location is very remote, approximately one kilometer from town. Walking is not recommended as it is situated at the end of a very dark and narrow partially dirt road which makes it virtually impassible by foot due to large water filled potholes.
There is only one restaurant and no shops within walking distance.
Housekeeping was also lacking when a request to change an accidentally soiled bedsheet came with a 500PHP fee.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
We had an emergency on our first night of stay here (water heater exploded upon plugging) affecting the eyes of my uncle. Upon report, we were shocked that they didn't have first aid kit. They didn't know what to do. What if it's a life at stake? No sense of urgency. The higher management should brief their staff for unexpected circumstances as this knowing that the nearest hospital  is an hour away from their hotel. 

after how many hours of reporting, finally, Kuya Rene (staff) offered to take my uncle to the hospital to have his eyes checked and shouldered expenses. 

Overall, this hotel needs more improvement. May this serve as a lesson to their management.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: The room was spacious but a bit old. The location is great and near to restaurants.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: It was a good experience for mu family tho there were so many insects inside their room. No toiletries and slippers provided inside the room too. Location is better than other hotels .

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
Too many marks on the walls (gives sense of poor maintenance and not clean room). Internal furniture is cheap and poorly mantained. Basic toilet  (hot water not functioning). Basic breakfast (white bread and egg).
It would be good for a budget hotel but I would expect more for the money paid.

reviews.title: Sick room.
reviews.text: Couldn't stay there within the first hour started coughing and wheezing because the smell of cleaning or mold scent.. had to sit outside till I called Agida to cancel rest of stay

reviews.title: Not fair charge
reviews.text: We had to pay extra for two matresses (₱1000/person/night) for two excess folks, one of which is a twelve year old. But they only gave us one extra matress and charged full amount for two folks. Location is secluded with so-so amenities. Free breakfast is just egg, coffee, and ham.

reviews.title: so dirty
reviews.text:
the property is dirty the location is ok..
the security guard is so kind and helpful.. but the property is disgusting
just clean the toilet would be good

reviews.title: Smoke all the time feeling suffocated the staff are good people.
reviews.text: First of all, many thanks to the wonderful staff who work there, they helped me a lot when I was sick, but... the place, the place... beyond the fence of the hotel, there is constant, very strong smoke, and with the heat, it is impossible to leave the room.  The rooms themselves..well...you will get what you paid for and those who understand will understand......in general I learned in the Philippines to take a hotel at least 4 stars and also 5 stars.  No choice.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: it is a nice cheap hotel for any one on a budget

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
The website said that there was a restaurant however it's closed for a while and it says 480 meters to Alona beach however it's been closed off need to walk much further .
The breakfast is very simple

reviews.title: It's  a value for the budget minded
reviews.text: AC worked well and room was clean except for the bathroom...black hair all over the shower  They were quick  to fix the shower head but water pressure  was extremely  low....we were on the 2nd floor.  I hope the owner invests in new cotton  towels instead of the polyester  rags provided.  Neither of us could dry ourselves  with the towels.  You get what you pay for.  Owner  and staff are very pleasant.

reviews.title: Conradas
reviews.text: Ok

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: If u are a seasoned traveller, and u just want a place to lie down after ur tours, Conrada's is good enough...but consider these: it is 3000++ steps away from McDonalds...going back from alona u might consider taking a trike(tuktok-100pesos)...the room (rm #5)has a kettle for hotwater ,but no ref...but the room is spacious... the place has 2 swimming pools whose water is source from rain ang spring water from the hills(it is clean!) Will rate Conrada's maybe 6-7/10...

reviews.title: Above average

During stay namin.ilan beses nawalan ng tubig.to the point na may sabon pa kami ni na nmin lumabas.
From 2nd floor need namin bumaba sa 1st floor para ituloy ang ligp namin.then naubusan din kami ng tubig.buti nlang nag dala bg mineral water ung isang staff.

reviews.title: Friendly hotel
reviews.text: It's a little out the way of Alona beach but it's very nice place to stay. Good for peace and quiet.  Breakfast is good. The owner conrada is very helpful.

reviews.title: Not Recommended
reviews.text: I’ll start with what was great about the place - their staff. Everybody was accommodating and friendly. But it was not enough to recommend this place to any of my family or friends. Pictures from the website were deceiving. The rooms were not clean. My family member killed two bugs the moment she stepped into her room. Ants everywhere. We had to keep asking for towels/supplies. The first night we had to use our own beach towels because they had no clean ones. Breakfast was just ok, not much to it. I don’t think that it was a cheap place so I can’t say you get what you pay for. It’s far from the beach so we had to get a ride to the beach or it’s a 15 minute walk. I wish I spent more money and stayed at a beachfront hotel. I actually payed to use another hotel facility by the beach to enjoy our trip to Alona Beach.

reviews.title: Staff was so accomodating ...
reviews.text: The room was no problem, staff was so accomodating , the place was quite far from beach you have to walk about 30 min, water in the room was salty, hard to adopt the taste specially f u make a toothbrush. Food was ok cheaper compare to outside stablishment. Sorry can't recommend for those who want to enjoy nd stay near the beach...

A bit disappointed at first, cause the guard is not knowledgeable of the guest list. Good thing Mrs. Conradas apologizes on his behalf.

reviews.title: Left early
reviews.text: The location is far from the beach. The tv did not work. The shower had no pressure. Booked for 4 nights but only stayed for one. No compensation was offered. Moved to a different hotel.

reviews.title: Hotel only for transit
reviews.text: The hotel is located in an unpleasant area, room with minimal comfort, old furniture. The hotel has no restaurant, not even the possibility to buy water, juices, sandwiches. The price per night reflects these shortcomings.

reviews.title: 그저 그렇다
reviews.text: 가격은 좋지만 골목이 무섭다

reviews.title: Not fancy, just enough
reviews.text: If you are looking for a budget place that is decent enough but no fancy facilities, then you may choose Conrada. Quite a walk from Alona beach, accessible nonetheless. Facilities are so-so but staff are generally nice. Somehow maintained, walls are repainted. Toilet though has a stubborn bidet and imperfect shower. Water in the pool is warm, nice for kids and oldies. Aircon is of lower capacity than is required for the room we got. Generally, 3.5/5.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Staffs are not around especially when needed at the dining area. Too far , need to barrow a scooter or a tricycle. Pool tiles is too slippery. Toilet seats are too high that cannot reach the floor. Not comfortable to seat on it. Aircon is too hot, need to clean or repair.  :-(

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: If you are traveling to Bohol, I suggest to look for accommodation near the beach. The location of this resort needs at least 10-15 minutes walk. The road has no light and might be dangerous for some tourist. The free breakfast is somewhat disappointing as it is just Hotdog or Longganisa with egg. They need to upgrade that if they want to compete with other resorts. Ants has easy access that even water they want to have. There is no lights in the pool area at night. Also, no umbrella as display in their photo. Toiletries is not enough for the guess. I would rate my stay as 2/5 as they need more improvement.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: The place is not near the beach (but it’s okay since price is affordable). The staffs are not accommodating. The hotel is somehow clean.

reviews.title: Honest review
reviews.text:
I want to start by saying that the staff is very very nice. But I have to give an honest review also.
This hotel is advertised as being 440 meters from the beach, it’s much farther.
There were no bath towels in my room and I had to ask for them three times before I got them and I didn’t get them until the next morning.
The sliding glass door that went to the porch was not lockable. I couldn’t get the TV to work at first and was told that the maintenance person was gone because of the holiday. If your going to rent rooms, then you should be providing service, even if it is a holiday.

reviews.title: What's better than Bohol?
reviews.text: Well, we love Bohol, but this wasn't the best location for a hotel.  It was down a long back road.  We weren't even sure we would find this place, even after asking for directions twice and still missing the turn-off.  It is a large place though and situated in two separate locations (near to each other).  The room was large enough and accommodating, but the door lock was difficult to close.  We had to have help once just to lock it up.  There were pools at both locations which were fine.  The dining area was small and located at the second location (main area) as was the check-in counter (so back and forth).  Other than that, we had no real complaint.  Stay there if you must or want a good price.  This was the best bang for the buck at the time.

reviews.title: Quiet and simple
reviews.text: This was a last minute decision trip to be able to join our travel buddies who were staying at a nearby resort which was fully booked.  So looked for a place nearby our buddies and ended up with this resort. If you’re just sleeping for the night and just need a place to freshen up then this is fine.   We got a family bunk room so enough for 2 adults and 2 older kids as they can stay in the double deck and we stayed in the Queen bed.  5 gallon water is provided in the room and there’s a refrigerator and a kettle  too with coffee, cream and sugar sachets.  Better bring your own tooth brush as only shampoo and soap is provided.  Gave feedback that they need to improve on the toilet fixtures but there’s water heater in the shower so goood enough.  The pool is not so inviting even if the water is clean because of the moss-like color effect.  Breakfast is continental and just ok but dont expect much.  They were very accommodating though when we asked if we could get packed breakfast as we needed to leave early for our island hopping and it was ready at 5:45 am. Plus plus:)  Oh well Bohol is indeed a 24-hour destination as you can go to Alona Beach anytime of the day-the beach that never sleeps these days…

reviews.title: Conrada’s
reviews.text:
Stayed 2 nights, had 4 rooms for my party with senior citizen, children and very young children. 
The owner, Conrada, along with her staff, personally met and checked us in when we arrived late in the evening. We’ve been interacting with her during our entire stay, like an old time neighbor.   This is quite rare in other accommodation.
The rooms are clean inspite the lean staff.
There’s a refrigerator and water-filled container w dispenser in each room. AC units are working well. 
Chose the place thinking that it’s just a walking distance to the highway and to Alona beach but was surprised that it’s actually at the innermost part of Brgy Tawala. We had to call for a motorcycle everytime we want to go somewhere.
A pool is a requisite for my kids. The pool at the main resort at first glance looked like it hadn’t been cleaned/filtered/treated . It’s actually due to the paint color they used for the bottom of the pool that give it it’s murky look inspite it being cleaned daily.
We just used the small pool at their annex. My kids had more fun there.
They only serve Continental breakfast.

reviews.title: Comfy stay
reviews.text:
Value for money: yes, definitely. We got our rooms with free breakfast and considering the size of the room, the water and toiletries provided, it is definitely value for money. 

Location: basing it on how near it was to Alona beach, it is around 15minutes of walk. Around 10 minutes to the busy center. Quite near from airport. Quiet neighborhood tho. 

Cleanliness: could use some improvement. While we understand that we booked this for a cheap price, I was not expecting a dusty bed frame and an electric fan full of dust. 

Staff: They are very helpful and accommodating. 

Facilities: heater in the shower is working well, ac unit is good too. However, some chairs are very dated and have to be replaced. We were bot able to swim but there was a pool in the standard annex. The lock to our unit is hard to operate/ needs fixing/replacement, if I may add. 

Overall, if you’re gonna be out all day. This will work for you. The rooms are spacious, even the shower and comfort room.

reviews.text: Conradas hotel is only a 15 minute walk from Alona Beach and is near to many tour companies, restaurants, bars, and massage places! There is actually only one pool (the rectangle one), which is nice to relax at for a day. Breakfast was a standard American breakfast. Staff were friendly and helpful for the most part. The one complaint I have is that we stayed there for four days with no one coming to clean our room, change our towels, replenish water bottles, etc. We had to ask several times before they finally cleaned our room. Apparently there is a little sign you’re supposed to put out, but our room didn’t have that. Anyways, overall a nice stay!

reviews.text: Our 1 night stay was great. One thing I just don't like was when we discovered that their building one doesn't have any generator we knew there will be a large brownout in the area by that time but we didn't expect that a three star resort can't provide a generator for their guest who will be staying in building 1 cause only their building 2 has the generator. so when it was brownout the time we checked in we dont have a choice but to stay at their standard room for the meantime until the electricity come back.  Their standard room is not cooling at all that's why its really inconvenient for us to stay there for like nearly 4 hours. Good thing the electricity came back earlier than expected since our baby is very sensitive if the weather is very hot.

reviews.title: I am not picky.
reviews.text:
We spent 4 nights and 5 days during the peak season with two families (5 people). 

I saw a post on Naver Blog saying that people were satisfied after bringing their kids, so I trusted it and went... Just now I learned that the comments on that blog post are blocked. This is my first time experiencing such accommodation. ^^ From 6 PM to 6 AM the next day, there's the sound of roosters crying, broken furniture, dim lighting... I'm really okay with all that. But the cleanliness... I found several cockroaches crawling inside a bag of candy. After having a beer on the table, within 10 minutes, cockroaches rushed out from under the table... If there are holes in the walls and floors of the accommodation, that's where the cockroaches come from... There's constantly powder coming out from the wooden furniture where cockroaches have been gnawing. The pool maintenance is lacking, and while having breakfast, a child from the family at the next table said... "I don't think even a cockroach would eat this." The reception is closed in the evening, so there's no response.
